prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with aging demographics

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Comprehensive fall risk assessment with gait/balance tests (TUG), orthostatic vitals, medication review, and home hazards evaluation

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; fall risk screening integrated in many geriatric pathways
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults at risk
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high where multifactorial programs are implemented

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Access to multidisciplinary geriatric assessment in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multifactorial interventions (exercise, home safety, deprescribing, vision correction, vitamin D where indicated)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Program implementation variability across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong evidence-based fall prevention programs in many systems
